programmes.show_description: The coppers with tiny choppers hunt for a man who assaulted an officer. And, in the dead of night, can the drone flush out a motorbike thief who's hiding in a wood?
Hannah & Mason's: Gordon Ramsay's rescue mission this time is Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in the quaint little town of Cranbury, New Jersey.
(2019) Supernatural horror. A nurse downloads an app which predicts that she'll be dead within days. As she uncovers its demonic roots, can she change her fate?
Just one hunt stands between the final four players and a prize of up to £100,000. But the last hunt brings a fiendish twist, big-money challenges - and the ultimate test of trust... (Ep9)
Lesley Perrett's on a mission to bring the Longwool sheep back from the brink of extinction. Landscape managers Steve and Jamie prepare the island of Tresco for a low-tide party.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: In Brighton, George helps Ododu, whose downsizing has left her with a kitchen quandary. And in Stockport, can Sarah make her childhood home work for modern family life?
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Where There's a Will: Sam grants a dying customer's wish to tend the bar for fun, but tensions mount when the man leaves a large sum of money to the patrons in his will.
Battle of the Exes: Carla asks Sam to be her plus-one for her ex-husband Nick's wedding to his glamorous new flame - which makes Nick jealous enough to want Carla back.
No Help Wanted: Norm's been out of work for a year and gets a job washing dishes - until Diane encourages Sam to hire him as his accountant to get his tax return completed.
Debra's Sick: With Debra ill, Ray juggles the kids and work, which causes problems when quarterback Terry Bradshaw agrees to have a meeting with him at the paediatrician's.
Who's Handsome?: Ray starts to feel insecure about his looks after hearing Debra compliment Robert on his appearance. Cue the fake tan.
The Car: Ray buys his parents' old car, but Debra refuses to drive it after finding out that Ray 'got lucky' in it as a teenager.
Don Juan in Hell (Part 1/2): As Frasier returns from a disastrous trip to Belize with his family, he faces the ghosts of past loves.
Don Juan in Hell (Part 2/2): Unsure about whether to end his relationship with his current girlfriend, Frasier is plagued with unwanted advice from his exes.
The First Temptation of Daphne: Daphne encounters the green-eyed monster when she learns that Niles is counselling an attractive patient who's obsessed with him.
The Return of Martin Crane: While Martin prepares for his first night's work as a security guard, Frasier, Niles and the gang fuss over who will have to walk Eddie.
Olde Hitching Post: Gordon visits the historic Olde Hitching Post restaurant in Massachusetts. Tom bought the business for his daughter but refuses to let her run it.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In the Cotswolds, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien unearth a mystery item that looks like a fish tank but turns out to have a different use entirely.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum & Dad
programmes.show_description: The Bakers find some wonderful objects in Beamish Open-Air Museum's storerooms, and Matt gets a crash course in driving a 1900s tram.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Sue and Albert love spending quality time on holiday with their children and grandchildren. Can Lee Juggurnauth find the perfect bolthole for them in their beloved Benalmádena for £190k?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: A VIP visits Paul and Fiona's villa in France. In Italy, Summer and Ashley decide to take on even more work. And in Portugal, Louise and Shay look for a new start.
The new series continues. Kathryn and Steve battle to revive their collapsing 14th-century kitchen. Can Becky and Mark complete grandad George's gîte in time for his 80th birthday?
...the Sun: The basement's almost complete, but the kitchen awaits. And Jasmine and Jon take a day trip to the local Roman baths to escape the dust and noise... Wishful thinking! (Ep10)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Censored Film Queens of Nigeria: Reporter Anja Popp meets the Nigerian women risking their livelihoods and reputations to push boundaries on screen, in a male-dominated world.
Battle of the Brands: From homeware to beauty to online, in a contest of penny-pinching power versus posh polish, how did Primark's bargains muscle in on John Lewis's bougie turf? (Ep3)
The Goggleboxers settle in for I'm a Celebrity... South Africa, Britain's Got Talent, The Dog House, The Apprentice, Secret Garden and the news on the Artemis II moon mission.
Sparks fly as First Dates returns. A former Lioness meets a cat lover. A yoga teacher contemplates a mindfulness coach. And a doctor calms her nerves with a self-confessed geek.
Amy Gledhill, Armando Iannucci, Joanna Page, Joel Dommett and Kumail Nanjiani leave all dignity at the door as they hunt for worms and feed Greg his five-a-day.
(2022) Fact-based comedy starring Bryan Cranston and Annette Bening as a retired couple who find a way to exploit a lottery loophole. But then some rich kids try to muscle in...
The Black Pearl: A struggling seafood restaurant in Manhattan, The Black Pearl, is hampered by debts, kitchen problems and three owners who all want to run things their way.
The competition kicks off at Greenways of Malvern in Worcestershire, where owner Geri Atterbury likes to provide a home from home, but a lack of en suite puts some guests off.
Sean and Maxine hope to give their rivals the royal treatment at The Royal Forester in Bewdley. Some of their guests are blown away by the rooms, but there's a hairy encounter for others.
Mum-and-son duo Sarah and Luke host at Ferry Lodge in Holyhead on Anglesey. But Sean and Maxine begrudge paying 50p for toiletries, and there's confusion over the 'Welsh' breakfast.
Sanjeev and Elinor welcome their rivals to Court Colman Manor in Bridgend. Not everyone feels the spark during a lesson in making fire.
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for one last time to settle some scores and find out how much they've been paid. Tensions run high before the winner is announced.
Kirstie Allsopp makes colourful recycled candles and a fun painted pebble game with her sons, and creates a host of cute feathered friends as she shows us how to make loo roll animals.
In Manchester, 'absolutely fabulous' Martyn and Russell, caravan fans Jane and Colin, and party lovers Rachel and Dave compete in a week when desserts are served via zip wire!
Screwed Driver: Doug's parents visit when Carrie is away, prompting Doug to act like a child. Meanwhile, Arthur invents a new type of screwdriver.
Lush Life: Carrie starts Doug on a new diet but goes easy on him if she's been out drinking with her friends. When she falls out with her pals, Doug keeps the alcohol coming...
Bun Dummy: Doug tries to stop Carrie wearing her hair in a bun because he wants her to look sexy at his school reunion. But how can he tell her without hurting her feelings?
Diamonds: Ray discovers that the diamond in the engagement ring he bought Debra is a fake, so he decides to get it changed without her knowing - a potentially expensive mistake...
The Game: When the family's cable television stops working, they resort to a game of Scruples - accompanied by inevitable arguments.
Recovering Pessimist: Ray changes his ways after Debra accuses him of being too pessimistic, but his nearest and dearest reckon he's become a bighead.
Love Stinks: Frasier hosts a party for the staff at the radio station to boost his popularity. Roz dates a waste collector.
Room Full of Heroes: Frasier's Halloween party takes an unpleasant turn when Niles drinks a few too many beers.
Bla-Z-Boy: An accident leaves a mess on Frasier's carpet and breaks Martin's chair. Niles gives piano lessons to a talented Roz and a not-so-talented Daphne.
The Day the Earth Stood Cool: Homer persuades a hipster couple to move in next door, but their friendship soon starts to deteriorate...
To Cur with Love: Homer loses Santa's Little Helper but doesn't seem to care, until Grampa tells the story of Homer's long-lost childhood pup Bongo.
Homer Goes to Prep School: Homer joins an off-the-grid survivalist group who have a top-secret doomsday bunker.
A Test Before Trying: When the school's threatened with closure because of low test scores, its fate rests with one student who missed the exam: Bart.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: After a long renovation, Tom and Terry trial-run their vegan B&B in Spain. And in Portugal, dreamers Louise and Shay find out if they can make it all a reality.
The B&B battle begins at the Mayfield Hotel in Seaburn, Sunderland. There's cause for applause when a seal makes an appearance, but some guests get picky about the paintwork.
The B&Bers descend on Gateshead for the week's second visit, at Sutherlands Hotel, where two guests want revenge and are determined to dig up some dirt.
Visit three is to Hugh and Loretta's Brampton Dales Farm in Lincolnshire. After an afternoon out at an old jail, the B&Bers feel sheepish about an uninvited guest at breakfast.
The week's last visit is to Valley View in Pendle, Lancashire where sisters Sharon and Deby hope the B&Bers get onboard with glamping. But one guest thinks his pod is like a student flat...
It's payment day, and after a competition full of controversial comments and divisive opinions, the B&Bers have plenty of scores to settle. But who's won the week?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Caterham, Ruth and Ken are torn over their town house. Can Phil's open-plan options and all-important garage pip Kirstie's persuasive plan to the garden post?
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
(2023) Seventh instalment in the frenetic sci-fi franchise. The Maximals - part animal, part robot - join the Autobots to battle against planet-munching Unicron and his Terrorcon cronies.
(2017) Fantasy adventure sequel with Dwayne Johnson, Karen Gillan, Kevin Hart and Jack Black. Four teenagers are sucked into the Jumanji video game to complete a dangerous quest.
(2023) Jennifer Lawrence stars as a woman hired for a summer to make a shy young lad a man before he heads off to college. Will there be any feelings in the end, hard or otherwise?
